title: CI: bloat checker: needs to handle multi-commit baselines
body:
[ Copied from [issue 14131](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14131#issuecomment-1119806582) ]:

>looks like the bloat check is not working correctly, https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14132 triggered this for some reason. I guess it uses the wrong base commit to compare to, it reabes 30 commits but the PR only has 8 commits in it. [log](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5208209660051456?logs=main#L499)

(Side note: the "30" is a distraction: `git rebase -x` multiplies everything by two. The real number is 15: 8 commits in that PR, and 7 in the previous one).

Analysis: `git rebase main^` (or whatever) does not mean what I think it means. Given this tree:
```
                            D (mybranch)
        x1 --- x2 --- x3   /
       /                \ /
A --- B ---------------- C    (main)
```
(where x1..x3 are three commits on a merged PR, and C is the merge commit, and D is the PR being submitted into CI).

You would think that `git rebase C^` would look like `B, C, D` but no, it looks like `x1, x2, x3, D`.

I've played with all command-line options and merge strategies, and can find no way to tell `git rebase` to start with `B`. I'm giving up for the day. Perhaps tomorrow will bring new insights.

comments.body:
Current idea: solve it by forgetting about stupid rebase behavior, and doing it this way in CI:
```diff
--- a/contrib/cirrus/runner.sh
+++ b/contrib/cirrus/runner.sh
@@ -257,8 +257,14 @@ function _run_altbuild() {                                                                                                                
             # requires a baseline, which is why we use '^' to start
             # with the *parent* commit of this PR, not the first commit.
             context_dir=$(mktemp -d --tmpdir make-size-check.XXXXXXX)
+            # Initialize sizes: push back to PR origin, run make, log sizes
+            savedhead=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
+            git checkout origin/$DEST_BRANCH
+            hack/make-and-check-size $context_dir
+            # pop back to PR, and run incremental makes, each checking sizes
+            git checkout $savedhead
             make build-all-new-commits \
-                 GIT_BASE_BRANCH=origin/"${DEST_BRANCH}^" \
+                 GIT_BASE_BRANCH=origin/"${DEST_BRANCH}" \
                  MAKE="hack/make-and-check-size $context_dir"
             rm -rf $context_dir
             ;;
```
Will submit PR in the morning because I don't work at this late hour.
